title: Eucapnos spectabilis
taxonomy: Eucapnos spectabilis is a species of flowering plant in the poppy family (Papaveraceae). It is native to the Balkan Peninsula. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Eucapnos, which includes a small number of species of flowering plants. Eucapnos spectabilis is further classified as Eucapnos spectabilis Lodd. ex Sweet, with its full name including the authority for its description and classification. intro: Eucapnos spectabilis, commonly known as the Spurred Coralroot or Spurred Coral, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Papaveraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it beneficial for wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Eucapnos spectabilis include alkaloids, phenolic compounds, and glycosides, such as spectabiline and proline, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in dried root form, allowing for its medicinal benefits to be harnessed for various health applications.
